suite.pass: 1. Quick check — do this every build

items.t: Nothing is broken when it opens
items.h: Open the game. It should show up normally — no error pop-ups, no blank/black screen.
From the page loading, it should take one click or tap (or none) to actually be playing. No menus in the way.

items.t: You can finish one whole round
items.h: Play a complete run/level/match from start to finish without getting stuck.
After you finish or lose, you can cleanly start again.
You can hear audio, and pressing mute actually silences it.
No hanging, no forever-loading, no screen you can’t get out of.

suite.pass: 2. First-timer test — do this when something is new

items.h: Pretend you’ve never seen it. Can you work out what to do just by looking at the screen?

items.t: No “wait… what do I do?” moments
items.h: Write down anywhere you got confused. That’s a spot to fix — it’s not you being slow.
Try things it might not expect — spam buttons, tap random spots. It shouldn’t break.

items.t: You always know where you are
items.h: At any moment you can tell: what to do now, what you just did, and what’s coming next.

suite.pass: 3. Check every feature — the thorough pass

items.t: Every button does what it says
items.h: Click each button/menu item. It should do exactly what its label promises.
Try keyboard, mouse/touch, and controller (if supported). Each should reach every action.
Earn some, spend some. Check the numbers change correctly and don’t glitch.
Close the game and reopen it. Whatever you unlocked or earned should still be there.
Check the edges: having zero of something, the maximum, and the very first time vs. later times.
Resize the window and try a phone-shaped screen. Nothing should overflow or get cut off.

suite.pass: 4. Is it fun and fair? — play a few rounds

items.t: Played a few rounds back to back
items.h: Do 3–5 full sessions in a row so you feel the real rhythm, not just one lucky or unlucky run.
Note any moment that felt cheap or unfairly hard, and roughly where it happened.
Note any stretch that dragged or felt repetitive. Boredom counts as a real problem here.
Would you start a 4th or 5th round? Jot down why or why not — that answer matters most.
Rate fun / fair / clear / would-recommend, each 1–5. Do it each build so you can see the trend.

suite.pass: 5. Ready for the store — do this before you submit

items.h: Run the platform’s own checklist. For CrazyGames that’s GAME_BIBLE.md in the play-area repo.
Not just your desktop browser — try it on an actual phone and/or tablet.
Try it with an ad-blocker on, slow internet, a backgrounded tab, and rotating the phone.

suite.pass: 6. Did a fix break anything? — do this after fixing bugs

items.h: Re-check everything near what you changed, not only the one bug you were chasing.
Do pass 1 again fully. A fix that breaks the basics is worse than the bug it fixed.
If you changed something lots of features rely on, test all of those features, not just one.

0:0.note: node --check on master + both regenerated alt builds; bot suite 12/12 wins, zero console errors

0:2.note: Combat rebalance: normal/fast/spear enemies take 2+ hits, wide clear gated behind combo≥4. Loop completes.

5:1.note: Verified via direct function calls in preview (hp decrement/stagger/lethal/empowered, brute shove-chain unaffected)

bugs.title: Counter-throw was OP — real fighting was skippable
bugs.actual: Every counter auto-cleared neighbors — FIXED (gate wide throw behind combo≥4; density cut ~30-40%)

debrief.q2: Mike playtest should sanity-check feel/difficulty before QA.

4:0.note: Second CG-guidelines audit — turned the same methodology on #036's own fixes

5:0.note: 12 more fixes; node --check on master + both alt builds; bot suite 11/12 wins, zero console errors

5:2.note: Live: zero SDK-throttle console errors (a compressed bot run had produced 56 before the debounce fix)

bugs.title: Shared SDK debounce silently ate happytime() on every win
bugs.actual: Shared gate treated happytime as a repeat — FIXED (independent timestamps)

bugs.title: GM/GD alt-build stubs used old ad-callback signature
bugs.repro:
1. Build for GameMonetize/GameDistribution
2. Trigger first ad
bugs.actual: startRun waited on a callback the stub never called → soft-lock. FIXED

bugs.title: First interstitial could fire on run #2
bugs.repro:
1. Start session
2. Finish run 1, start run 2
bugs.expected: No ad before a real 3-min wait
bugs.actual: adT started at 0 — FIXED (adT = session load time)

bugs.title: Mute button clickable while hidden under pause overlay

debrief.q2: Several of these were regressions introduced by JF-#036 — the audit turned on its own prior fixes.

4:0.note: First CG-guidelines audit — ran GAME_BIBLE checklist against the code

4:2.note: Portrait mobile was a bare 375x211 letterboxed strip (74% black bars) with no rotate prompt — FIXED this batch

5:0.note: 12 fixes applied; bot suite 10/12 wins (duck:jump hit ratio improved ~5:1 → 2:1)

bugs.title: Ad request fired in same tick as gameplayStart()
bugs.repro:
1. Finish run
2. Start next when ad due
bugs.actual: SDK saw gameplay "active" during an in-flight ad — FIXED

bugs.title: No rotate prompt on portrait mobile
bugs.actual: Tiny letterboxed strip, no explanation — FIXED

bugs.title: Duck dodge window shorter than jump

0:0.note: 600-frame all-silly smoke (every joke scroll + cosmetic + Butcher boss): no error

0:2.note: Loop completes with all 10 "make it sillier" gags active

3:1.note: bot fairness = 12/12 wins, 7 hits all dodgeable throws, 0% unreactable, 0% pincer

debrief.q4: 10 gags all built on existing systems.

0:5.note: startRun clears darts + resets jump/duck + grounds player — no stuck state

2:0.note: FULL TEST BATTERY (headless, 17/17): dodge windows generous, wrong-height fails correctly, 52f telegraph + 18-32f reaction budget, attack timing, spacing sane

3:1.note: BOT FAIRNESS re-run: 12/12 wins, 3 hits (all dodgeable darts), 0% unreactable, 0% pincer

bugs.title: Duck pose read as "short", not "crouching"
bugs.repro:
1. Duck during a fight
2. Look at the player
bugs.actual: Uniform squash only read as "short" — FIXED: explicit horse-stance pose + torsoDrop + ground shadow

0:2.note: Duck/jump dodge axis + spearman throws added; loop still completes

3:1.note: FAIRNESS (?bot=3&react=12): 11/12 wins, 14 hits (13 darts + 1 melee), 0% unreactable, 7% pincer. Harder + still fair.

3:0.note: One legit Rooftop w3 loss (the spear district) — intended difficulty, not a bug

debrief.q4: Difficulty bump per Mike "campaign too easy": dBase 1/3/5/7/9, boss hp 3/4/5/5/6.

0:2.note: Bot fairness suite (?bot=3&react=12, ~200ms reaction): 12/12 wins, avg wave 5.0

0:5.note: Zero hits across 12 runs; no livelock after bot fixes (no longer pokes brutes/bosses, waits on body-block, dismisses ending)

3:1.note: No unreactable telegraphs at normal speed. Stress ?bot=2&react=18 (300ms): 8/8 wins, 4 hits all intended

debrief.q4: Perfect-play bot wins 20/20 — difficulty leans gentle at optimal play; viper floor is 16f (267ms) if late-game ever feels cheap.
